title: machine cache might not be working with podman-remote
body:
### Issue Description

It seems like the podman-machine-os is downloaded every time?

`quay.io/podman/machine-os:6.0`

`podman-machine.x86_64.qemu.qcow2.zst`

Is there something amiss with the cache, when using the binary?

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. podman-remote-static machine init
2. podman-remote-static machine rm
3. podman-remote-static machine init

https://github.com/podman-container-tools/podman/releases/download/v6.0.0/podman-remote-static-linux_amd64.tar.gz


### Describe the results you received

downloading again

### Describe the results you expected

using the cache dir

### podman info output

Podman 6.0.0
Ubuntu 22.04
x86_64

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

qemu

### Additional information

~~Seems like this is related to the "static", in `podman-remote-static`:~~

`DEBU[0003] Error creating a SQLite blob info cache at /home/anders/.local/share/containers/cache/blob-info-cache-v1.sqlite, using a memory-only cache: beginning transaction: Binary was compiled with 'CGO_ENABLED=0', go-sqlite3 requires cgo to work. This is a stub`

EDIT: The error was a red herring, it is not related to this artifact blob

comments.body:
With `podman-remote` (dynamic), it worked OK:

`DEBU[0003] Using SQLite blob info cache at /home/anders/.local/share/containers/cache/blob-info-cache-v1.sqlite`

But it still downloaded the whole 1.0G blob again.

`DEBU[0004] Compression change for blob sha256:5bbc6f483dc80d26c3f78e28855e2d68c87e47ea1b13c0385b70fa515d91f640 ("application/octet-stream") not supported `

I noticed the same on my mac when I was testing something earlier the cache there no longer works either.

Note the blob info cache is unrelated and should not be used here as there is custom logic for the machine image pulls

cc @baude

It seems like "cache" is always false, since df4905d68bc8fd8318bdac0d28ccd435fac1a0d7.

pkg/machine/ocipull/ociartifact.go

```go
        cache := false
```

If I change it to true, it starts caching...

`DEBU[0002] cached image exists and is latest: /home/anders/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/qemu/cache/ec9187248ec786976870e816cc13ad1c41f56f10c0fc0fd248dc59ff0bb76491.qcow2.zst`

The cache key is the manifest, not the artifact.

```json
{
  "schemaVersion": 2,
  "mediaType": "application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json",
  "config": {
    "mediaType": "application/vnd.oci.empty.v1+json",
    "digest": "sha256:44136fa355b3678a1146ad16f7e8649e94fb4fc21fe77e8310c060f61caaff8a",
    "size": 2,
    "data": "e30="
  },
  "layers": [
    {
      "mediaType": "application/octet-stream",
      "digest": "sha256:5bbc6f483dc80d26c3f78e28855e2d68c87e47ea1b13c0385b70fa515d91f640",
      "size": 1097728249,
      "annotations": {
        "org.opencontainers.image.title": "podman-machine.x86_64.qemu.qcow2.zst"
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

Both with podman-remote and podman-remote-static
I tried to remember why it uses ~/.local/share/containers/.../cache and not ~/.cache/containers/...
